title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · History Brief: Snapshots Graph
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git snapshots graph increment in a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.text: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 7.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. A defensible solution applies Snapshots Graph, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 4
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
